$comment: Source of truth for the apodictic:style_label block (Interpretable Stylometric Explanation). One block per measured stylistic feature (or feature cluster) that the descriptive overlay glosses with natural language, as a reading aid ON TOP of the Author Voice Fingerprint (#9, apodictic.voice_fingerprint.v1). It does NO new stylometry: it CONSUMES the SETEC voice_profile per-family feature inventory (families.<family>.top_features[].{feature, mean, sd, cv}) and adds only a natural-language LABEL bound by provenance to the measured feature it describes. The module is strictly DESCRIPTIVE — it NAMES a measured feature, it NEVER prescribes a voice change and never fabricates a style claim. The shape is built so a 'write more like X' directive cannot be expressed: there is no target/goal/recommendation/rewrite/compare_to_author field, `frame` is a closed enum that admits only descriptive stances, `direction` is a closed within-author indicative enum (NOT an instruction), and every label carries a `feature_ref` provenance into a consumed measurement (presence + non-emptiness checked in Python, X2, the anti-fabrication gate — the #9 centroid_ref discipline applied per-feature). `feature_family` is a closed taxonomy mirroring SETEC's families. `magnitude` is a closed band (marked/moderate/slight). The label carries NO Must/Should/Could editorial severity (a style observation is not a defect; X3 rejects any severity leak or an embedded apodictic:finding block). `register` is a comparability class (same role as #9's register) used only to keep a describes-cluster claim within one register (X5) — never 'be like author Y'. The profile is local-only (no external call; X6 advisory hygiene). The subset schema engine (apodictic_artifacts.validate_obj) honors only required/const/enum/type/array-minItems+item-type/pattern; the firewall's schema half is carried by those keywords. Residue (the X4 label-prose prescription scan, feature_ref non-emptiness, X5 same-register comparability) is enforced in Python — the content_advisory.py precedent where the prose-vs-label override split lives in code because the engine cannot express it. Validated by scripts/style_explanation.py. See docs/interpretable-stylometric-explanation.md.
title: APODICTIC interpretable style label (apodictic.style_label.v1)

feature_ref.$comment: Provenance: names the consumed SETEC voice_profile feature this label glosses (e.g. 'families.function_words.top_features[the]'). Presence + non-emptiness checked by X2 in style_explanation.py — the external value cannot be re-resolved here; the module consumes, it does not compute (the #9 centroid_ref limit). Not in `required` const-checks beyond `type` because X2 owns the non-emptiness gate (the spec separates X1 schema from X2 provenance).

feature_tokens.$comment: Optional: the concrete tokens the feature ranks over (e.g. ['the','and','but']). Descriptive evidence for the label; carries no directive.

label.$comment: The natural-language gloss on the measured feature. The single free-text surface; the X4 firewall gate scans it for a prescriptive voice-directive or a comparison-to-emulate construction. The engine cannot enforce maxLength — a length cap, if ever needed, is a Python check (A5).

register.$comment: Comparability class (same role as #9's register). Used only to keep a describes-cluster's referenced labels within one register (X5) — never a target author to emulate.
